WITH yearly AS (
    SELECT c.Segment,
           strftime('%Y', ym.Date) AS year,
           SUM(ym.Consumption) AS total
    FROM yearmonth ym
    JOIN customers c ON ym.CustomerID = c.CustomerID
    WHERE c.Currency = 'EUR'
      AND ym.Date BETWEEN '2012-01-01' AND '2013-12-31'
    GROUP BY c.Segment, year
),
perc AS (
    SELECT y1.Segment,
           (y2.total - y1.total) / y1.total * 100.0 AS pct_increase
    FROM yearly y1
    JOIN yearly y2 ON y1.Segment = y2.Segment
    WHERE y1.year = '2012' AND y2.year = '2013'
)
SELECT 'biggest' AS type, Segment, pct_increase
FROM perc
ORDER BY pct_increase DESC
LIMIT 1
UNION ALL
SELECT 'lowest' AS type, Segment, pct_increase
FROM perc
ORDER BY pct_increase ASC
LIMIT 1;	----- bird -----	debit_card_specializing

SELECT
    CASE WHEN a.male_count > b.male_count THEN a.Admission ELSE b.Admission END AS more_category,
    CASE WHEN a.male_count > b.male_count THEN a.male_count ELSE b.male_count END AS more_count,
    CASE WHEN a.male_count > b.male_count THEN a.male_percent ELSE b.male_percent END AS more_percent,
    ABS(a.male_percent - b.male_percent) AS deviation_percent
FROM (
    SELECT Admission,
           COUNT(*) AS male_count,
           COUNT(*)*100.0/NULLIF(COUNT(*),0) AS male_percent
    FROM Patient
    WHERE SEX = 'Male'
    GROUP BY Admission
) a
JOIN (
    SELECT Admission,
           COUNT(*) AS male_count,
           COUNT(*)*100.0/NULLIF(COUNT(*),0) AS male_percent
    FROM Patient
    WHERE SEX = 'Male'
    GROUP BY Admission
) b

WITH
    female_clients AS (
        SELECT client_id, (julianday('now') - julianday(birth_date))/365.25 AS age
        FROM client
        WHERE gender = 'Female'
    ),
    max_age AS (
        SELECT MAX(age) AS max_age FROM female_clients
    ),
    oldest_females AS (
        SELECT fc.client_id
        FROM female_clients fc
        JOIN max_age ma ON fc.age = ma.max_age
    ),
    accounts_of_oldest AS (
        SELECT d.account_id
        FROM disp d
        JOIN oldest_females of ON d.client_id = of.client_id
    ),
    avg_salary_per_account AS (
        SELECT account_id, AVG(amount) AS avg_salary
        FROM trans
        GROUP BY account_id
    ),
    lowest_avg_oldest AS (
        SELECT MIN(avg_salary) AS min_avg
        FROM avg_salary_per_account
        WHERE account_id IN (SELECT account_id FROM accounts_of_oldest)
    ),
    highest_avg_overall AS (
        SELECT MAX(avg_salary) AS max_avg
        FROM avg_salary_per_account
    ),
    gap AS (
        SELECT (max_avg - min_avg) AS salary_gap
        FROM highest_avg_overall, lowest_avg_oldest
    )
SELECT a.account_id, g.salary_gap
FROM accounts_of_oldest a
JOIN avg_salary_per_account asp ON a.account_id = asp.account_id
JOIN lowest_avg_oldest lao ON asp.avg_salary = lao.min_avg
JOIN gap g;	----- bird -----	financial
